fields.title: A Home
fields.subtitle: Luxurious holiday accommodation on a working farm, nestled in the rolling countryside five miles from Tunbridge Wells.

fields.title: Where Countryside
fields.paragraph1: Set in the heart of the Kentish Weald, Albans Barnyard is a small working farm raising pedigree Jacob sheep and North Devon Red Ruby cattle among rolling orchards and ancient woodland.
fields.paragraph2: Cox Cottage has been lovingly designed with sustainability at its heart — powered by ground source heat pump, solar panels, and heat recovery ventilation — without compromising on luxury. Dijon limestone floors, a hand-built kitchen, and a wood burning stove create the perfect retreat.
fields.paragraph3: Whether you're watching lambs in spring, tasting our own honey in summer, or curling up by the fire in winter, every season brings something special.

fields.title: Lambing Season
fields.subtitle: Watch our Jacob ewes and their lambs live on the Lambcam, or book a spring stay and experience it first-hand.

fields.title: Lambcam
fields.description: Watch our flock in real-time. During lambing season, you can witness the arrival of new lambs from the comfort of your screen. Our Jacob sheep have been a beloved feature of the farm for generations.

fields.title: Cox Cottage
fields.description: A beautifully restored barn conversion with two spacious ensuite bedrooms, an open-plan kitchen and living area with wood burning stove, and a private garden overlooking the farm. Designed with sustainability and luxury in equal measure.

fields.address:
Albans Barnyard
Romford Road
Pembury, Kent
TN2 4BB
fields.directions: We're tucked away down a farm track, so satnav can be unreliable. From Matfield village, take the B2160 south. After passing The Poet restaurant, take the first left into Foxhole Lane, then immediately left again into Romford Road. Follow the track to the farm.
fields.directionsTip: Tip: Use postcode TN12 7JH for The Poet, then follow our directions from there.
